raw_content.homepage: --> Prix Versailles | Official Website top of page THE WORLD ARCHITECTURE AND DESIGN AWARD “Inspiration, Progress and Inclusion” Laureates Area Prix Versailles About Nominations Contact International Appeal Press Editions Videos Geography Press release PRIX VERSAILLES WORLD CEREMONY 4 December 2025 | Paris, UNESCO 2025 Laureates A PRIX VERSAILLES INITIATIVE: RECOGNISING THE “WORLD'S MOST BEAUTIFUL” ACHIEVEMENTS The "World's Most Beautiful” Achievements recognition, granted by the Prix Versailles, highlights the primary role of the Laureates in beautifying and improving the living environment. These achievements, awarded each year by the Prix Versailles, are examples presented to the world, signs of exceptional architecture and design. #BeautyEverywhere 2025 Laureates Editions ANNUAL EDITIONS 2015 - 2025 About ABOUT THE PRIX VERSAILLES Architecture and intelligent sustainability Announced each year at UNESCO since 2015, the Prix Versailles is a series of architectural competitions that shine a light on the finest contemporary projects worldwide. At the intersection between multiple disciplines, the members of the Prix Versailles World Jury are tasked with awarding twenty-four World Titles from among the Selected Sites – in the categories of Airports, Campuses, Passenger Stations, Sports, Museums, Emporiums, Hotels and Restaurants. The Official List – which pays tribute to innovation, creativity, reflections of local heritage, ecological efficiency and the values of social interaction and participation, which the United Nations holds in high regard – is in line with the principles of intelligent sustainability, taking the projects’ ecological, social and cultural impacts into consideration. “Dialogue and interconnections between the different aspects of the human environment – from ecology to social relations to culture – pave the way for intelligent sustainable development. More than a mere goal, this offers a wonderful promise of the ability to meet the latest challenges facing the world today.” Jérôme Gouadain Secretary General of the Prix Versailles Geography GEOGRAPHY 2017 -
